name: Tour Aotearoa - South Island
description: The South Island section of Tour Aotearoa with larger terrain and remoter sections.
difficultyExplanation: South Island TA adds bigger weather exposure, longer remote connectors, and tougher climbing blocks. Riders need stronger self-sufficiency than on flatter, town-linked routes.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Lewis Pass to Murchison section is the biggest climbing day, often compounded by westerly headwinds. Resupply gaps stretch up to 80 km in places.
routeSummary: Tour Aotearoa - South Island is a hard bikepacking route covering about 1,446 km, commonly ridden over 12 days. The South Island section of Tour Aotearoa with larger terrain and remoter sections. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Nov-Mar, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Lewis Pass to Murchison section is the biggest climbing day, often compounded by westerly headwinds. Resupply gaps stretch up to 80 km in places. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 12-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
